Fatn Polrrrc,rl Pnacrrcss C ouvrrssroN
P.O. Box lt07 . ll8 J Strect . Sacranrento. CA 95li I2-01J07
(916) 122-566() . Fax (916) -l2l-0ltfi6
January 24,2001
Ollie M. McCaulley
40329 Giles Lane
Palmdale, CA 93551
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 01-007
Dear Mr. McCaulley
This is in response to your request for advice regarding provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").r As we understand matters, you are seeking advice on portions
of the Act which you are litigating in a civil action brought by a creditor in Los Angeles
County Municipal Court. From a subpoena you recently served on the Commission, it
appears that this matter is set for trial next week, on January 30, 2001.
Courts are fully competent to interpret the provisions of the Act, and will properly
apply the law to the facts proven at trial after a fuIl and fair hearing. The Fair Political
Practices Commission cannot know what facts may be proven in court, and we decline to
provide written advice on matters that appear to be past conduct and which can, in any
event, be more fully and competently adjudicated by the court next week. Our decision
to defer to the trial court is based on Regulation 18329(bX8) - copy enclosed - and this
letter serves as the notice required under that regulation, which provides in full:
"(8) Subsection (a) notwithstanding, the Commission may
decline to give formal written advice. The requestor shall
be notified if the request for formal written advice is
declined. Formal written advice may be declined in any of
the following circumstances :
(A) The requestor is seeking advice relating to past
conduct.
' Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.


,
File No. 01-007
Page No. 2
(B) The requestor is seeking advice about another person's
duties under the Act and has not been authorized to do so
by that person.
(C) It appears that the material facts provided in the
request may be inaccurate, incomplete, or in dispute.
(D) The request does not present a question under the
Political Reform Act, or is purely hypothetical.
(E) The request presents issues requiring a policy
interpretation best made through a Commission Opinion
under Government Code Section 83114(a) or by adoption
of a regulation pursuant to Government Code Section
83tt2.
(F) A response rendering formal written advice would be
inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest."
If the court requests the FPPC's assistance, we will of course provide the court
any aid it may require.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
Acting General Counsel
r4ful-L
By:
Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

January 26,2001
Ollie M. McCaulley
40329 Giles Lane
Palmdale, CA 93551
Dear Mr. McCaulley:
This is to acknowledge receipt of your voicemail message this morning, advising
me that the court has continued your hearing from January 30, 2001 to February 16. I
have alerted Mr. Moen on this development, and asked him to change his calendar
accordingly. I look forward to speaking with your attorney in the near future, and thank
you for your courtesy in promptly notifying us of this schedule change.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Acting General Counsel
By

Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

February 2,2001
Scott MattinglY
Law Offices of Scott MattinglY
9828 Charleville Blvd.
Beverly Hills, CA 90212
Dear Mr. MattinglY:
I write to follow up on the voicemail message I left you severai days ago relative
to the McCaulley action. I understand that the trial has been continued to February 16,
2001. please serve Mr. Moen with a subpoena reflecting the date, time and place where
he will be required, no later than one week prior to the appearance date'
I have also confirmed that the earlier subpoena (for an appearance on January 30)
was not accompanied by the payment required under Government Code Sections 68097.1
and 68097.2. In order for Mr. Moen to bL properly served for the February 16 trial, the
subpoena must be accompanied by the payment required by the statutes noted above.
To assist you with your computations, I include the estimated costs to this agency
reimbursable under the above statutes. This estimate presumes a moming appearance'
requiring Mr. Moen to travel to Los Angeles the aftemoon before the hearing
5435.67 DailY Salary (1.5 daYs)
194.92 Air fare
86.90 Hotel
49.80 Car rental (plus gas and tax)
34.00 Meals
8.00 Incidentals
$809.29


I
tI
File No. 01-007
Page No. 2
please serve your subpoena on this office at your earliest convenience, so that we
may arange for Mr. Moen's "br.n... If you have any questions, if the matter settles, or
if you othJrwise choose not to go forward, please call me on my direct line, at (916) 322-
5744.
SincerelY,
Luisa Menchaca
Acting General Counsel
By:
Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion: The FPPC declined to provide written advice, citing Regulation 18329(b)(8) on the grounds that the requestor is seeking advice relating to past conduct and that material facts may be in dispute, deferring instead to the competence of the trial court. The FPPC indicated it would provide assistance if the court requested it.
sections.question_synthetic: Mr. McCaulley sought FPPC advice on provisions of the Political Reform Act that are the subject of his litigation with a creditor in Los Angeles County Municipal Court, with trial scheduled for January 30, 2001.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice on the grounds that the requestor sought guidance on past conduct in a pending litigation matter best resolved by the trial court, citing its regulatory authority to decline formal written advice in such circumstances.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: File contains three related letters from January-February 2001. Primary response is the January 24 declination letter with full regulatory citation. Subsequent letters address scheduling changes and subpoena procedures with cost estimates for witness appearance. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; reasoning extracted from declination letter body.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Mr. McCaulley sought FPPC advice on provisions of the Political Reform Act that are the subject of his litigation with a creditor in Los Angeles County Municipal Court, with trial scheduled for January 30, 2001.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide written advice, citing Regulation 18329(b)(8) on the grounds that the requestor is seeking advice relating to past conduct and that material facts may be in dispute, deferring instead to the competence of the trial court. The FPPC indicated it would provide assistance if the court requested it.

embedding.summary: This correspondence file contains three letters from the FPPC to Mr. McCaulley and his attorney regarding his litigation-related request for advice on the Political Reform Act. The FPPC declined the request, citing regulatory authority to decline advice on past conduct in disputed matters, and subsequently provided administrative coordination regarding trial scheduling and witness appearance requirements.
